meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Design with AI to Solve No-Code Barriers
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between vision and production. Use this intermediate guide to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS faster.
content.answer_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your roadmap. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can translate high-fidelity AI designs into production-ready SaaS codebases instantly. This workflow bypasses traditional coding barriers, allowing you to iterate on business logic while the AI handles the syntax.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with brilliant ideas that never saw the light of day because the founder was held hostage by technical complexity. You have the vision, the market insight, and the drive, yet the 'no coding experience' wall feels like an insurmountable barrier to your first dollar of MRR.

But the rules of the game have changed. Today, you don't need a computer science degree to build a scalable product; you need an architectural mindset and the right AI leverage. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a passive dreamer into a high-velocity AI Builder capable of deploying production-grade software in hours, not months.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational database schema and core API endpoints to ensure your product is scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Next.js 14 architecture with Tailwind CSS and Supabase integration. Define a schema for a subscription-based SaaS with user authentication, protected routes, and a plan-tier system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold the base project using the defined schema and initialize the Supabase client'

steps.title: Design High-Fidelity UI Components
steps.description: Generate modular, responsive UI components that prioritize conversion and user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-converting landing page and a clean user dashboard. Use Lucide icons, responsive grid layouts, and ensure all components are reusable and accessible.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the dashboard layout and connect the sidebar navigation to the main content area'

steps.title: Implement Business Logic
steps.description: Integrate complex logic such as payment processing or AI-driven tasks using structured code generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a server action for Stripe integration. Include logic for handling webhooks, updating user subscription status in Supabase, and redirecting users based on their plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add Stripe webhook handler and create the subscription checkout server action'

steps.title: Perform Automated Testing
steps.description: Use AI to write unit tests for your critical paths to ensure stability before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Playwright tests for the user authentication flow and the subscription checkout process. Focus on edge cases like failed payments and invalid logins.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and execute unit tests for the authentication and payment modules'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Streamline the CI/CD pipeline to push your MVP to a live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a production-ready Vercel configuration and environment variable template for a secure SaaS deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit security vulnerabilities and prepare the project for deployment to Vercel'

pro_tips: Always define your data schema before generating UI; AI hallucinations are minimized when the backend structure is locked in first.
Use Claude Code to perform a 'security audit' on your generated code before pushing to production to catch potential dependency vulnerabilities.
Treat your AI prompts like a PRD (Product Requirements Document) to avoid ambiguous output; be specific about frameworks and naming conventions.
When stuck on a bug, use Claude Code to read the specific error log file rather than pasting the entire codebase to save context tokens.
Build in 'feature flags' early so you can toggle experimental AI-generated modules without breaking your stable production core.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering user feedback for 48 hours, then return to this workflow to ship your first feature update based on real usage data.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same technical stack used by top-tier SaaS founders. The only difference between you and a successful exit is the speed of your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
